evals.expected_output: Runs git status/diff, stages files, writes a conventional commit message, commits without pushing
expectations: A git commit was created in the repository
The commit message follows conventional commits format (starts with feat:, fix:, chore:, docs:, refactor:, test:, or similar)
git status or git diff was examined before staging files

evals.prompt: I've updated the auth middleware and added some tests — can you commit this?
evals.expected_output: Reads diff, stages relevant files, writes a meaningful commit message describing the auth middleware change
expectations: A git commit was created in the repository
The commit message references the auth middleware or tests
Both the middleware file (middleware.js) and the test file (middleware.test.js) were staged

evals.expected_output: Recognises this as a commit request, stages and commits without pushing
expectations: A git commit was created in the repository
The agent interpreted 'save my work' as a request to commit (not just save files to disk)
